title: Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica
what_description: Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica is a plant species within the family Papaveraceae, native to the Qinghai-Xizang Plateau in China. It is a perennial herb with a height range of 10-30 cm, characterized by a basal rosette of leaves and a flowering stem with a pair of bracteoles. The leaves are oblong-elliptic, 5-10 cm long, and have an entire margin, while the flowers are pale yellow, with a 4-lobed calyx and a 4-petaled corolla. The fruit is a capsule, 1.5-2.5 cm long, containing numerous seeds. The plant blooms from July to August, and its flowers are pollinated by bees and other insects.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica are pain, inflammation, and anxiety, which is alleviated by its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and anxiolytic properties. The plant's ability to reduce inflammation also helps with arthritis and digestive issues, while its adaptogenic properties aid in stress and insomnia. Furthermore, Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica has been shown to effectively manage depression, fatigue, muscle spasms, migraines, fibromyalgia, fever, nausea, and headaches due to its ability to modulate neurotransmitters and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the plant's antispasmodic properties help alleviate muscle spasms, and its anti-inflammatory properties aid in treating gastrointestinal issues and osteoporosis. Overall, the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica also aid in treating chronic pain and infection, and its sedative properties help manage sleep disorders.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica are its antioxidant effects, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage. Its anti-inflammatory properties enable the plant to reduce inflammation and alleviate associated pain, making it a potential treatment for various conditions. The analgesic properties of this plant allow it to relieve pain by acting on the nervous system and interrupting pain signals. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms. Furthermore, its antispasmodic properties enable the plant to relax and calm muscle spasms, reducing their frequency and intensity.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica are alkaloids, specifically corydaline, a major isoquinoline alkaloid, tetrahydroberberine and tetrahydropalmatine, both isoquinoline alkaloids with analgesic properties, tetrahydroprotoberberine, a protoberberine alkaloid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory activity, and corynoline, an alkaloid with a potential analgesic effect, all of which contribute to the plant's therapeutic properties. These compounds have been isolated and identified through chromatography and spectroscopy techniques, and their structures have been elucidated. The presence of these alkaloids in Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica is responsible for its traditional medicinal use in treating pain and inflammation. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds have been demonstrated in in vitro and in vivo studies, supporting their potential therapeutic applications. The unique combination of these alkaloids in Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica may contribute to its efficacy in treating various health conditions.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica are corydalis tincture, a concentrated liquid extract used to enhance the plant's bioactive compounds, corydalis tea, a warm beverage made by infusing the dried plant material in hot water, corydalis infusion, a liquid extract made by steeping the plant in cold water for an extended period, corydalis decoction, a liquid extract made by boiling the plant material in water to release its bioactive compounds, and corydalis salve, a topical preparation made from the plant's roots and leaves mixed with a carrier oil, which may be used to treat skin conditions. The choice of preparation depends on the desired application, with tincture and decoction often used for medicinal purposes and tea and infusion used for relaxation. Corydalis tincture is typically standardized to contain a specific amount of the plant's active compounds, such as yohimbine and corydaline. The plant's roots and leaves contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The salve is applied topically to affected areas, allowing the bioactive compounds to penetrate the skin and exert their therapeutic effects.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica improperly include experiencing nausea, which can be a result of its emetic properties, as well as suffering liver damage due to its potential toxicity and ability to cause hepatotoxicity. Additionally, individuals may experience dizziness, which can be attributed to its alkaloid content and potential interaction with the central nervous system. The alkaloids in Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica, specifically tetrahydropalmatine, can also cause liver damage and other adverse effects when ingested in large quantities or for extended periods. Furthermore, its improper use can lead to severe dizziness, which may impair an individual's ability to perform daily activities safely. Prolonged or excessive use of Corydalis brevirostrata subsp. tibetica can exacerbate these side effects, emphasizing the importance of proper dosing and medical supervision when using this plant.
